message.content:
Yes, several large language models (LLMs) were publicly available before ChatGPT in 2022. Some notable ones include:

GPT-3 (2020): Developed by OpenAI, GPT-3 was widely known and available through OpenAI’s API before ChatGPT. It was a major predecessor to the models used in ChatGPT.

BERT (2018): Developed by Google, BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers) was widely adopted for natural language understanding tasks, though it was more tuned for specific tasks rather than conversational generation.

T5 (2019): Also by Google, the T5 (Text-to-Text Transfer Transformer) model was designed for a wide range of NLP tasks.

XLNet (2019): A generalized autoregressive pretraining method, building on BERT, also used in multiple NLP tasks.

These models were either available via APIs, research papers, or open-source repositories and were used for various NLP applications prior to the broader launch of ChatGPT in late 2022.
